name: EMU
tagline: The white US spacewalk suit — every American EVA from the Shuttle to the ISS
description: Extravehicular Mobility Unit — modular EVA suit with a hard upper torso, soft fabric arms and legs, and a Primary Life Support System backpack. Operates at 4.3 psi pure O₂. Originally fielded for Space Shuttle EVAs in 1981 and still in service on the ISS more than 40 years later. SAFER mini-thruster pack added after Challenger for tether-loss rescue (1.4 kg N₂, ~3 m/s Δv).
best_known_for: The white US spacewalk suit that has flown every American EVA from the Shuttle through the ISS
